data.reasoningText:
The class has stub methods that throw NotImplementedException. I need to write a complete MSTest test suite for this class. Since the methods are not implemented yet, the tests will define the expected behavior. Let me use the code-testing-generator agent to generate comprehensive tests.

Actually, since the methods all throw NotImplementedException, these tests will fail when run against the current code. But the user asked me to write tests for the class based on its API. I should write tests that define the expected behavior, and note that the implementation needs to be filled in for the tests to pass.

Let me create a proper MSTest test project and write comprehensive tests. I'll need to:


1. Set up a test project with the necessary references
2. Write tests that cover the core functionality: adding items with SKU, quantity, and price; removing items by SKU; calculating the total cost; and applying discount codes

Based on the method signatures, I can infer the expected behavior and write tests that will serve as a specification for the implementation.

  mkdir -p src/Store tests/Store.Tests && \
  mv ShoppingCart.cs src/Store/ && \
  dotnet new classlib -n Store -o src/Store --force -f net9.0 --no-restore -q && \

data.content:
Clean build, zero errors. Here's a summary of the test suite:

**`tests/Store.Tests/ShoppingCartTests.cs`** — 20 tests across 4 areas:

| Area | Tests | What's covered |
|---|---|---|
| **AddItem** | 9 | single item, multiple qty, multiple SKUs, duplicate SKU merging, null/empty SKU, zero/negative qty, negative price |
| **RemoveItem** | 4 | remove existing, remove one of many, non-existent SKU, null SKU |
| **CalculateTotal** | 2 | empty cart → 0, add+remove combo |
| **ApplyCoupon** | 5 | valid code reduces total, null/empty code, invalid code, total never negative, coupon on empty cart |

> **Note:** All methods in `ShoppingCart` currently throw `NotImplementedException`, so every test will fail until you implement the class. The tests serve as a behavioral specification for the implementation.
